Can you please confirm the inactivity times currently set (and are they different for candidate/interviewer)?

The corpus describes the Leave / End Interview controls available in the top right corner: interviewers can use the "Leave Interview" option to leave without disturbing others, or use "End Interview for all" to end the session for all participants.

The Virtual Lobby feature includes an Interviewer Lobby and Candidate Lobby where participants can wait and see real-time status of all participants. Interviewers can admit candidates from the lobby or move candidates back to the lobby without ending the interview.

However, the corpus does not document specific inactivity timeout durations (like the ~20 minute timeout your interviewers are experiencing), whether inactivity timeouts differ between candidates and interviewers, or configuration options to extend inactivity timeouts for your account. Issue:
Hello! I am trying to remove an interviewer from the platform. I am not seeing this as an option when I select the three dots next to their name.
Can you let me know how to do this?
Subject: How to Remove a User
Response:
To remove an interviewer from the platform, you have two options depending on your goal:

**Option 1: Remove from a specific team**
If you want to remove the user from a specific team:
1. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account
2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner
3. Select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu
4. Go to the **Teams** tab
5. Select the team you want to update
6. Select the **Users** tab
7. Locate the user in the list
8. Select the **delete** icon in the **Action** column

**Option 2: Remove from Teams (account level)**
If you want to remove the user from teams at the account level:
1. Go to **Teams Management** > **Users** tab
2. Click the **three-dot icon** next to the user's name
3. Select **Remove from Teams**
4. Confirm the new owner (if the user owns any resources like tests, you'll need to transfer ownership)

You can also use **Bulk User Management** to remove multiple users from teams at once by selecting users and using the dropdown menu for bulk actions.

If the three-dot menu is not showing the expected options, ensure you have admin permissions to manage users.

Source: 1508181842 teams management, Manage Team Members, Bulk Add/Remove Users to Teams
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus documents both team-level user removal (delete icon in Action column) and account-level Remove from Teams option (three-dot menu), plus bulk user management capabilities. Next: user follows the appropriate removal procedure based on whether they want to remove from a specific team or from teams at the account level.

Issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
Subject: Subscription pause
Response:
HackerRank offers a **Pause Subscription** feature that allows you to temporarily pause your subscription for 1 to 12 months instead of canceling it. However, this feature is only available for specific plan types.

**Prerequisites for Pause Subscription:**
- You must have a **monthly subscription** for one of the following plans:
  - Individual Monthly - Basic
  - Interview Monthly
- Your subscription must have been active for at least 30 days

**If your plan is eligible, here's how to pause:**
1. Click on the **profile icon** in the top-right corner and select **Settings**
2. Navigate to the **Billing** section under **Subscription**
3. Click the **Cancel Plan** button
4. The UI will display a prompt about the **Pause Subscription** feature
5. Select your desired pause duration (minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months)

You can extend the pause period at any time, even during an active pause, by returning to the Billing page.

**If your plan is not eligible for pausing** (for example, if you have a team or enterprise plan), you can still cancel your subscription at any time. Cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing cycle, and you'll continue to have access to your plan features until the cycle ends. At the end of the cycle, your account will revert to the Basic plan.

If you need assistance determining your plan type or require alternative options for pausing a team or enterprise subscription, a support agent can help.

Source: Pause Subscription, Cancel Subscription Plan Cancellation options Cancelling subscription plan
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus documents the Pause Subscription feature with clear prerequisites (individual monthly plans only) and the general cancellation procedure as an alternative; user's context suggests possible team plan but the documented self-serve procedures are actionable if they have an eligible plan. Next: user follows the pause procedure if they have an eligible individual monthly plan, or contacts support if they need assistance with a different plan type.

Issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
Subject: Certificate name update
Response:
Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. You can update the name on your certificate only once per account. When you update your name, the change applies to all your certificates. After you update it, you cannot change it again.

To update the name on your certificate:

1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the name you want to display.
3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**.

The platform will update your certificate with the new name.

Source: Certifications FAQs
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly documents the certificate name update procedure with step-by-step instructions and the one-time-only policy. Next: user follows the four-step procedure to regenerate their certificate with the correct name.

Issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
Subject: Website Data crawl
Response:
Anthropic uses web crawlers for model development and other purposes, and you can block them from accessing your website using standard robots.txt directives.

To block Anthropic's crawler from your entire website, add this to the robots.txt file in your top-level directory (do this for every subdomain you wish to opt out from):

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

To limit crawling activity rather than block it entirely, Anthropic supports the non-standard Crawl-delay extension to robots.txt. An example:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

Anthropic's crawling principles include:
- Transparency in data collection using identified bots
- Minimal disruption by being thoughtful about crawling speed and respecting Crawl-delay
- Honoring industry standard "do not crawl" signals in robots.txt
- Respecting anti-circumvention technologies (e.g., not bypassing CAPTCHAs)

Source: Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly documents Anthropic's web crawling practices and provides exact robots.txt directives to block ClaudeBot from accessing websites. Next: user adds the provided robots.txt rules to their website's top-level directory and any subdomains they wish to protect.

Issue: I am facing multiple issues in my project. all requests to claude with aws bedrock is failing
Subject: Issues in Project
Response:
When all requests to Claude with AWS Bedrock are failing, here are two common causes to check:

1. **Regional availability**: Visit the Amazon Bedrock user guide (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/models-regions.html) to see the Claude models that are available by AWS Region in Amazon Bedrock. Ensure your AWS region supports the Claude model you're trying to use.

2. **Model access**: Get started with Claude in Amazon Bedrock by visiting the Amazon Bedrock console (https://console.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/). For a step-by-step walkthrough on how to request Claude model access in the Amazon Bedrock console, view this blog: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/anthropics-claude-3-5-sonnet-model-now-available-in-amazon-bedrock-the-most-intelligent-claude-model-yet/

If you've verified both regional availability and model access and requests are still failing, contact AWS Support (https://aws.amazon.com/contact-us/) for Claude in Amazon Bedrock support inquiries or reach out to your AWS account manager. For community-based support, visit AWS re:Post (https://repost.aws/).

Source: What AWS Regions are Claude models available in Amazon Bedrock?, How do I get access to Claude in Amazon Bedrock?, I use Claude in Amazon Bedrock. Who do I contact for customer support inquiries?
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus covers the two most common causes of Bedrock request failures (regional availability and model access) plus the AWS Support escalation path for persistent issues. Next: user checks region configuration and model access in Bedrock console, then contacts AWS Support if issues persist.

Issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
Subject: Employee leaving the company
Response:
To remove an employee who has left from your HackerRank hiring account:

1. Log in to your **HackerRank for Work** account using your credentials.
2. Select your profile icon in the upper-right corner.
3. Select **Teams Management** from the drop-down menu.
4. Go to the **Teams** tab.
5. Select the team that includes the employee.
6. Select the **Users** tab.
7. Locate the user in the list.
8. Select the **delete** icon in the **Action** column.

If the employee owned any resources (such as tests), you may want to transfer ownership before removing them. To transfer ownership:

1. Go to **Teams Management** > **Users** tab.
2. Locate the user whose resources you want to transfer.
3. Click the more options menu under the **Actions** column.
4. Select **Transfer Ownership**.
5. In the **Transfer Resource Ownership** dialog, confirm the new owner.

Alternatively, you can lock the user's access, which removes them from all teams and prevents account access. To lock a user:

1. Go to **Teams Management** > **Users** tab.
2. Select one or more users.
3. Click **More** and select **Lock**.
4. Confirm in the **Lock Users** dialog.

Source: Managing team members, Transferring resource ownership, Locking user access
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus provides complete step-by-step procedures for removing team members, transferring resource ownership, and locking user access—all directly applicable to removing a departed employee. Next: follow the delete icon procedure in Teams Management > Teams > Users tab to remove the employee from the team.

Issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
Subject: Claude for students
Response:
To set up a Claude LTI key for your students in Canvas, you'll need to work with your Claude for Education administrator and Canvas LMS administrator. Here are the steps:

**In Claude for Education (Administrator):**
1. Sign in as an administrator
2. Navigate to Organization settings > Connectors
3. Find Canvas and click "Enable"
4. In the settings modal, input:
   - Canvas Domain
   - Client ID (found in Canvas Admin -> Developer Keys)
   - Deployment ID (found in Canvas Admin -> Settings -> Apps -> View App Configurations -> Claude LTI Settings Button -> Deployment ID)
5. Click "Save Changes"

**In Canvas (Installing the App):**
1. Go to Admin -> Settings -> Apps
2. Click "View App Configurations" then select "+ App"
3. Select Configuration Type "By Client ID"
4. Input the Client ID generated for your developer key
5. Click "Install" and refresh the course page

These steps require both Claude for Education administrator access and Canvas LMS administrator access. If you have questions about your Claude for Education plan account or the Claude LTI, contact your university's administrator(s).

Source: Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus provides complete step-by-step procedure for setting up Claude LTI integration in Canvas, including both Claude for Education organization settings and Canvas app installation. Next: user follows the documented steps with their Claude for Education administrator and Canvas LMS administrator to enable the integration.

Issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
Subject: Visa card minimum spend
Response:
The merchant is correct. In the US Virgin Islands and other US territories (like Puerto Rico and Guam), merchants are permitted to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for Visa credit card purchases. This is an exception to Visa's general rule that merchants cannot establish minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions.

However, this exception applies only to credit cards. If you are using a Visa debit card, the merchant cannot require a minimum purchase amount. Similarly, if you have a credit card but the merchant is requiring more than US$10 as a minimum, that would violate Visa rules.

If the merchant is requiring a minimum on a debit card, or requiring more than US$10 on a credit card, please notify your Visa card issuer.

Source: Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?
Justification: Decision: replied. Why: corpus directly addresses minimum spend requirements in US Virgin Islands, confirming the $10 minimum is permitted for credit cards in US territories but not for debit cards. Next: user now understands the rule and knows to contact their card issuer if the merchant violates it (debit card or >$10 minimum).
